title: DFIR-IRIS
description: Open-source collaborative incident response platform for DFIR analysts to share investigation evidence, tasks, and timelines.
installation: DFIR-IRIS is a multi-container stack; this template runs the primary web/app server (ghcr.io/dfir-iris/iriswebapp_app) only. You MUST deploy these companion services to make it functional: (1) a worker from the SAME ghcr.io/dfir-iris/iriswebapp_app image with env IRIS_WORKER=1 (it runs the Celery jobs handler and shares this app's env, the postgres add-on, and the rabbitmq add-on broker). Upstream also ships (2) ghcr.io/dfir-iris/iriswebapp_db (a pre-tuned Postgres image) and (3) ghcr.io/dfir-iris/iriswebapp_nginx (a reverse proxy that terminates TLS on 443 and proxies to the app on 8000). Here the Kubero ingress terminates HTTP and the postgres add-on replaces the db image, so neither companion image is required; deploy iriswebapp_nginx only if you need its static-asset routing. Pin all companion images to the SAME tag as this app (v2.4.20). IMPORTANT: rotate IRIS_SECRET_KEY and IRIS_SECURITY_PASSWORD_SALT (each 'openssl rand -hex 32') and the postgres/rabbitmq passwords before production. The app auto-creates the schema and an initial 'administrator' user on first boot; the generated admin password is printed in the app pod logs (grep for 'WARNING :: post_init :: create_safe_admin'). User-uploaded case evidence is stored in the database, but server-side datastore files live on the mounted /home/iris/downloads volume.
